verses.text: 'Behold, I will send my messenger'; 'the Lord, whom ye seek, shall suddenly come to his temple'; but 'who may abide the day of his coming?' The Forerunner and the Lord. Christ comes to His temple, preceded by John.

'Behold, I will send my messenger'; 'the Lord, whom ye seek, shall suddenly come to his temple'; but 'who may abide the day of his coming?' The Forerunner and the Lord. Christ comes to His temple, preceded by John.
He shall sit 'as a refiner... of silver,' purifying the sons of Levi to offer in righteousness. Refining grace. God purges His people that their worship may please Him.
He shall sit 'as a refiner... of silver,' purifying the sons of Levi to offer in righteousness. Refining grace. God purges His people that their worship may please Him.
He will judge the sorcerer, adulterer, false swearer, and oppressor; 'I am the LORD, I change not.' Unchanging God. His constancy is both the sinner's fear and the saint's hope.
He will judge the sorcerer, adulterer, false swearer, and oppressor; 'I am the LORD, I change not.' Unchanging God. His constancy is both the sinner's fear and the saint's hope.
'Return unto me, and I will return'; 'Will a man rob God? Yet ye have robbed me... In tithes and offerings.' Withheld dues. To keep back from God what is His is a kind of robbery.
'Return unto me, and I will return'; 'Will a man rob God? Yet ye have robbed me... In tithes and offerings.' Withheld dues. To keep back from God what is His is a kind of robbery.
'Bring ye all the tithes into the storehouse... and prove me now... if I will not open you the windows of heaven.' A promise to test. God invites His people to trust His generous return.
'Bring ye all the tithes into the storehouse... and prove me now... if I will not open you the windows of heaven.' A promise to test. God invites His people to trust His generous return.
He will 'rebuke the devourer' for their sakes, and all nations shall call them blessed. Blessing assured. Obedience opens the door to God's protecting and prospering hand.
He will 'rebuke the devourer' for their sakes, and all nations shall call them blessed. Blessing assured. Obedience opens the door to God's protecting and prospering hand.
Their words were 'stout' against Him; they said, 'It is vain to serve God,' and called the proud happy. Bitter complaint. Hard hearts begrudge God and envy the wicked.
Their words were 'stout' against Him; they said, 'It is vain to serve God,' and called the proud happy. Bitter complaint. Hard hearts begrudge God and envy the wicked.
Their words were 'stout' against Him; they said, 'It is vain to serve God,' and called the proud happy. Bitter complaint. Hard hearts begrudge God and envy the wicked.

But they that feared the LORD spoke together, and 'a book of remembrance was written'; 'they shall be mine... when I make up my jewels.' Treasured ones. God records and will reward those who fear Him.
